name: Global truncation error
statement_text:
Assuming that f satisfies a Lipschitz condition, with
constant L, the global error satisfies the bound
                   
                   
                    y(x0 )− y (x0 ) + Hm(x−x0 ),          L = 0,
   y(x) − y(x) ≤ exp((x−x0 )L) y(x0 )−     y (x0 ) + (exp((x−x0 )L)−1) Hm
                                                                         L ,
                   
                                                            L > 0.
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
The theorem bounds the global error \(y(x)-\widehat{y}(x)\) for a numerical method applied to an ODE.  
Variables: \(f\) (ODE function, Lipschitz constant \(L\)), \(y(x)\) (exact solution), \(\widehat{y}(x)\) (numerical approximation), \(H\) (max step size), \(m\) (bound on local truncation error), \(x_{0}\) (initial point).  
Referenced equations:
\begin{align*}
(212a)&\quad \alpha(x)=\|y(x)-\widehat{y}(x)\|,\\
(212b)&\quad \beta(x)=\alpha'(x),\\
(212c)&\quad E(x)=\int_{x_{0}}^{x}\beta(\xi)d\xi,\\
(212d)&\quad |\beta(x)|\le L\alpha(x),\\
(212e)&\quad \alpha(x)=E(x)+\int_{x_{0}}^{x}\beta(\xi)d\xi.
\end{align*}

variables.name: f
variables.definition: the function defining the differential equation, assumed to satisfy a Lipschitz condition with constant L

variables.name: y(x)
variables.definition: the exact solution of the differential equation

variables.name: ŷ(x)
variables.definition: the numerical approximation to the solution

variables.name: H
variables.definition: maximum step size used in the numerical method

variables.name: m
variables.definition: a bound related to the local truncation error, likely defined earlier in the subsection

equations.content: likely defines α(x) = ||y(x) - ŷ(x)|| or similar global error measure
likely relates β(x) to α(x), possibly β(x) = α'(x) or a difference
likely gives an expression for E(x) or a bound involving m
likely gives a bound like |β(x)| ≤ L α(x)
likely an alternative expression for α(x) involving β and E

preamble: The theorem provides a bound on the global error y(x) - ŷ(x) for a numerical method applied to an ODE. The analysis uses a function α(x) measuring the error, a bound m for local truncation errors, and a maximum step size H. The preceding text derives inequalities leading to this bound by considering cases L=0 and L>0.

dependencies.name: Lipschitz condition in its second variable
Global truncation error bound via local error accumulation
